Party_Details.Name: Lim Suat Hua

Facts.Text: Lim Suat Hua was appointed as Executive Director of Singapore HealthPartners
Lim's Service Agreement stipulated a monthly salary of $60,000
A Settlement Agreement was executed, terminating Lim's employment effective 25 July 2010
Lim began receiving a reduced salary of $50,000 per month
Maurice Choo proposed to Lim a salary reduction to $50,000
Board meeting minutes recorded a resolution to offer Lim a New Service Agreement with a reduced salary
Lim's employment with Singapore HealthPartners ended

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the plaintiff is entitled to claim unpaid salary based on the original terms of her service agreement
Party_Details.Rule: A party may claim unpaid salary if there is no valid agreement or estoppel preventing reliance on the original contract terms
Party_Details.Application: No valid agreement existed to reduce her salary, while an estoppel by convention prevented her from asserting the original salary terms
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that an estoppel by convention prevented Lim from claiming the full unpaid salary, but allowed her claim for pro-rated salary for July 2010.

Party_Details.Name: Singapore HealthPartners Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Singapore HealthPartners was incorporated
Lim Suat Hua was appointed as Executive Director
Board resolved to offer Lim a New Service Agreement with a reduced salary
Maurice Choo communicated a proposal to Lim to reduce her salary
The Settlement and Share Purchase Agreement was executed
Lim's salary was reduced to $50,000 per month
Lim's employment with Singapore HealthPartners ended

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the defendant is legally responsible for unpaid salary based on the original service agreement terms
Party_Details.Rule: A company is bound by the terms of a service agreement unless a valid variation or estoppel is established
Party_Details.Application: An estoppel by convention prevented the plaintiff from claiming the original salary, while no valid variation occurred
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that an estoppel by convention applied, limiting the defendant's liability for unpaid salary, but required payment of pro-rated salary for July 2010.

Party_Details.Name: Singapore HealthPartners Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Lim took a trip to Thailand at Singapore HealthPartners' expense from 2008-10-01 to 2008-10-03
Lim took a trip to New Zealand at Singapore HealthPartners' expense from 2008-10-20 to 2008-10-24
Wizvision provided information technology services to Singapore HealthPartners from around November 2007 to around September 2010
Lim failed to disclose her 15.59% indirect interest in Wizvision held through Hua Jie Pte Ltd to the board of Singapore HealthPartners

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the defendant by counterclaim contravened management obligations by failing to disclose interests and making unauthorized trips
Party_Details.Rule: A director must disclose any personal interest in company transactions and avoid unauthorized actions that could harm the company
Party_Details.Application: Lim's failure to disclose her interests and unauthorized trips contravened her duties, the trips were related to disclosed transactions
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the unauthorized trips were related to disclosed transactions, but Lim's failure to disclose her interest in the third Wizvision contract was not covered by the release.

Party_Details.Name: Lim Suat Hua

Facts.Text: Lim travelled to Thailand from 2008-10-01 to 2008-10-03 to review the implementation of Amalga hospital software for the project
Lim travelled to New Zealand from 2008-10-20 to 2008-10-24 to study the architectural, mechanical, and electrical engineering aspects of a new hospital building in Auckland
Lim did not fully disclose her 15.59% indirect stake in Wizvision through Hua Jie Pte Ltd before Singapore HealthPartners engaged Wizvision
The Settlement Agreement dated 2010-07-12 included Clause 10.7, which waived claims arising from disclosed transactions

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the plaintiff by counterclaim is legally responsible for failing to disclose interests and making unauthorized trips
Party_Details.Rule: A director is required to disclose all interests in company transactions and ensure all actions are authorized and beneficial to the company
Party_Details.Application: The trips were related to disclosed transactions and that her failure to disclose was an oversight, while the trips were unauthorized and interests undisclosed
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the trips were related to disclosed transactions and that the release clause covered the first two Wizvision contracts, but not the third.
